answer:
\boxed{B}

The study found no significant differences in knee extensor/flexor strength, hamstring/quadriceps ratio, or position sense between bicompartmental knee arthroplasty and TKA at any time point. While both groups improved in physical performance, only the TKA group showed enhanced stair climbing, and neither group demonstrated superior outcomes for muscle strength or most performance metrics. Thus, bicompartmental arthroplasty was not more favorable overall.
